Lord Śiva Saves the Universe by Drinking Poison

Lecture Transcription

tamutthitaṁvīkṣyakulācalaṁpunaḥ
samudyatānirmathituṁsurāsurāḥ
dadhārapṛṣṭhenasalakṣa-yojana-
prastāriṇādvīpaivāparomahān

Translation

When the demigods and demons saw that Mandara Mountain had been lifted, they were enlivened and encouraged to begin churning again. The mountain rested on the back of the great tortoise, which extended for eight hundred thousand miles like a large island.

surāsurendrairbhuja-vīrya-vepitaṁ
paribhramantaṁgirimaṅgapṛṣṭhataḥ
bibhrat tad-āvartanamādi-kacchapo
mene ’ṅga-kaṇḍūyanamaprameyaḥ

Translation

O King, when the demigods and demons, by the strength of their arms, rotated Mandara Mountain on the back of the extraordinary tortoise, the tortoise accepted the rolling of the mountain as a means of scratching His body, and thus He felt a pleasing sensation.

Purport

The Supreme Personality of Godhead is always the unlimited. Although the Supreme Personality of Godhead, in His body as a tortoise, held on His back the largest of mountains, Mandara-parvata, He did not feel any inconvenience. On the contrary, He apparently felt some itching, and thus the rotation of the mountain was certainly very pleasing.

Translation, O King, when the demigods and demons, by the strength of their arms, rotated Mandara Mountain on the back of the extraordinary tortoise, the tortoise accepted the rolling of the mountain as a means of scratching His body, and thus He felt a pleasing sensation.

trai-guṇya-viṣayāvedā
nistrai-guṇyobhavārjuna
nirdvandvonitya-sattva-stho
niryoga-kṣemaātmavān

Krishna is the controller of three modes of material nature. In the Bhagavat Gita he explains

mattaḥparataraṁnānyat
kiṣcidastidhanaṣ-jaya
mayisarvamidaṁprotaṁ
sūtremaṇi-gaṇāiva

That there is no truths superior to me, everything on is resting upon me as pearls are strung on the tread. The supreme lord is absolute; he is beyond the capability of material senses to comprehend. One who is unfortunate tries to comprehend the infinite, inconceivable nature of the Lord, by trying to relate his glories and pastimes according to his mundane faculty of reasoning such a person is sure to be utterly bewildered, because the lord in his achintayasakti, everything he does is inconceivable to our material mind and senses...

ataḥśrī-kṛṣṇa-nāmādi
nabhavedgrāhyamindriyaiḥ
sevonmukhe hi jihvādau
svayamevasphuratyadaḥ

One can never understand Krishna or perceive him through mundane sense perception, it is only when the mind and senses are utterly spiritualized can we perceive the beauty and glory of supreme personality of godhead. It is said in Brahma Samhita that

premāṣjana-cchurita-bhakti-vilocanena
santaḥsadaivahṛdayeṣuvilokayanti
yaṁśyāmasundaramacintya-guṇa-svarūpaṁ
govindamādi-puruṣaṁ tam ahaṁbhajāmi

That only when one one’s eyes are smeared with salve of love can one can see the beautiful form of shyamsundar. Similarly only when one’s heart is decorated with the divine ornaments of faith can one begin to comprehend or understand infinite glories of the supreme personality of godhead. This Earth planet is approximately twenty five thousand miles around according to the modern scientists. And as you know up until just the past five hundred years in the recorded history a western science, western culture no man was ever able to conquer this great deed of circumambulating this world. You know Kristopher Columbus that very famous navigator he was trying to circle the world but because they had such little inform about tiny this little Earth planet he did not really know where he was going. He just thought that if I go farther enough West I end up at India, because he speculated this earth must be round. And of course on the way he came into this large land what he thought was India, but actually it was America. So it is said he discovered America. Although there were millions of Indians of course they were not called Indians then but there were millions of natives living very peacefully in that tract of land. So Kristopher Columbus never actually achieves his goals going around the world, it was one a I believe a Portuguese seller of the name Mazelion and It was he that finally went down to the bottom of the South America, Argentina and went around and ultimately he circled the world. This was considered very very great historical event. So we think how many years these people living in the western world and up until very recently no one had ever been able tocircled the tiny globe which is twenty five thousand miles. Twenty five thousand miles considered such a huge amount of distance, but here we read that the supreme lord. He appeared in the form of tortoise and he extended the length and breadth of his shell eight hundred thousand miles, Sometimes you know on the roads we see this tiny little tortoises very small we think ohh... they are so nice looking. Children they go.... How charming they are to look at little tortoises. In America one of the most famous pet is little turtles this little one baby little. They never grow up bigger than this in whole life. And they feed little turtles and let them crawl-on them and its a very very enjoyable experience. And then when you go to place like KesiGhat or RadhaKund,specially a place like KeshiGhatbor some ghats on vrindavanand then you see big big tortoises and he thinks my god so big People are afraid to go in the water tortoise are so big. They are astonished especially many westerners they used to have a pet little turtle. When they see this tortoise in the Yamuna many of them will not go in. They will think my god so big. How big are they? Their shell may be at the most... how big? What do you think? 3 feets..what? 10 feet !!!tortoise? I would say two feet at the most. Ten feet where did you see a tortoise ten feet in Yamuna?He must see... he must have transcendental vision, because we only see them being at least two feets at the most three feet. But here we find that when the supreme personality of godhead Narayana takes form of tortoise, he is eight hundred thousand miles ... Can you imagine? Hnn... all the spaceships and rocket ships they could never in their whole lives goes through one end of tortoise to others. So when Krishna does something he does not do things like you and me. Sometimes just to show as an example he plays the role of an ordinary child but then again just to show the difference he performs some superhuman activity. Which is most inconceivable, just like when sri Krishna lifted the goverdhan hill he was only a child of the age of seven. And here was a large mountain which today is twenty one kilometres in size to walk around. And those days of course it was much bigger and the residence of vrindavanthey were thinking that no... it is not possible Krishna cannot lift this hill? They were putting their sticks under the hill and they were thinking actually they were holding it up. Because Krishna he was not like a big wrestler, big muscle and extravagant physique, no he was just a very simple little cowherd boy, his limbs were very tender and very soft. So they could not comprehend how was it possible that how could he lift the hill, its not possible, we better make sure it does not fall on him because it might hurt him. So they all were putting sticks under the hill and they were working very hard to hold the hill on. And Krishna was simply smiling on them accepting the ... the devotional attitude of their willingness to help and assist him. And Krishna although he lifted the hill for seven days he felt not even the least inconvenience.

Similarly we read of how lord srikrishna in expansion of anantsesh is holding the universe on the hoods of his transcendental head, he feels no inconvenience, can you imagine the weight of universe, so the lord his strength is not like the strength of ours. The Mandaramountain which is so incredibly heavy and difficult for even the most mighty conquerors, the demigods and demons. Although it was being grind it was being rotated back and forth on the back of this great tortoise. You were think that there would be some inconvenience or some pain but Kurmadeo was feeling very very pleasing, scratching sensation. Just like there is saying you scratch my back I will scratch yours. When you have itch on the back its... itsvery .....it feels very good when someone scratches it. And since you cannot scratch yours own back because you are such a limited jivattama that you cannot even scratch your own back and you think you goanna conquer the whole world, you think so great you cannot...... you need someone to scratch your back, its so helpless so when someone comes and scratch your back you think, very nice... very nice. But if they scratch too hard you say no..no not so hard.... not so hard. But Kurmadev they were scratching his back by having this mountain, which was thousands of miles high made out of solid gold which all demigod and all the demons could not lift.And it was being grind it was grinding upon his back and he was thinking very nice... very nice. This is Krishna ... Krishna appears in this wonderful ways just to attract our minds, after all he did not have to appear like tortoise. Simply by his mere desire he could have supported this great mountain of mandara. Which was sinking on the ocean but because god is a person he loves to exhibit extraordinarily beautiful pastimes for his pleasure and for the pleasure of his devotees. Now why would the impersonal Brahman if the lord is impersonal, ultimately without a personality, without a sense of humour, why in the world would he just decide to appear in as an eight hundred thousand miles tortoise. Why Brahman is an infinite energy of the lord why not just by that energy support the mountain unh... will be no problem. Just by will that mountain would been supported. But because Krishna is a veryvery transcendental personality and he has wonderful sense of humour and has a incredible variegated propensity of enjoyment and giving enjoyment to his devotees that perform such wonderfulwonderful pastimes that human being could never be imagine even though the greatest expert, science fiction writer could never come up with story like this.Could they? Have you ever heard? Whether churning a mountain of gold in an ocean of milk. Demons and demigods and the rope is a huge gigantic serpent by the name Basuki. And as they are churning the mountain starts sinking into the ocean and then trruuhh... trruuhh.... the hero comes to rescue and eight hundred thousand miles big, he just crawl underneath and he thinks aunh... how nice this scratching mountain against my back. Who could possibly think story like this even thethe extent of human imagination has never come up like anything. So do you think that some impersonal energy can possibly exhibit such divine extraordinary activities? No we read about Krishna in vrindavan, we read about how transcendentally humorous he is? How he is always very playful? And how he is always most anxious to please his devotees in so many ways? Just like in vrindavan just to please his devotees Krishna would be in his cowherd pastured ground and they would see a peacock. And Krishna you want to see me imitate a peacock and he would start walking just like a peacock and all he friends would be laughing ha..ha..ha.. Krishna you look just like a peacock, yesyesharibol !!! and then they would even see monkey jumping from tree Krishna says you want to see me jumping from tree and he would imitate monkey from tree, cowherd boys says yeah.... you look just like a monkey from tree. They all starts laughing and clapping their hands. In this way Krishna, his only business is vrindavan is to give pleasure to his devotees. Last night we were hearing a story of when srimatiradharani became angry at Krishna for various transcendental reasons Krishna even makes srimatiradharani angry just to increase her pleasure and srimatiradharani becomes angry just to increase his pleasure. And then he he went through the forest of gabrivan to collect peacock feathers and decorated his body and started dancing like most graceful and beautiful peacock, just to appease the anger of srimatiradharani. And she saw him she thought unh such a wonderful peacock, he is my shyamsundar, let me dance with him. So she also collected the peacock feathers decorated her body and they danced together. So you see Krishna in the supreme abode he has the most wonderful transcendental intelligence. So in that intelligence in spiritual world he is always thinking newer and fresher ways to give pleasure to his devotees to enjoy the reciprocation of the rasa of loving exchanges. So it is that supreme lord srihari. It is that giridhari of vrindavan who is original source of cause of all causes and it is he that is controller of all material and spiritual worlds. Imagine the artistic ability of srikrishna, we were talking about how limited science, fiction writers are compared to the type of story that Krishna can think of what is to speak of artist, the best artist is an artist who can,back some hundred years ago during the BOROK (20.20) western art the best artist the artist who is most realistically paint, exactly what makes it really look like. It’s like in Italy the greatest artist Leonardo da Vinci, Michalengeloif you see their carving and you see their paintings. It’s unbelievable how realistic these forms are? People say my god how can anyone paint with brush, manifest such works of art that look exactly like the real thing, with such expression. They are considered good artist, because they can copy some little scene. What are they really creating? They are not creating anything. They are actually just duplicating to some extent with the little their own personality being expressed and of course modern art they don’t like doing anything realistic they like everything look like impersonalistic thingunrealistic. Where they just have all kinds of crazy, I don’t even know what they call it, Modern art. It really just the idea of the modern art is to, is to defy the real sense of form and personality and just create an illusory sense of beauty is donedesigned and coloured. But even these modern artists, basically all their painting is the strangeness of their own minds. People think it is very beautiful does not mean anything, more meaningless it is more they consider it artistic. It’s like very famous peace of arts, major piece of art in one particular city in Americaand people from all over the United States, people from all over the world come to see it and what is it? It’s a gigantic six story high orange colour cloth pin. You know what cloth pin is? When you hang a cloth in a line, you take a little pin and you clip on to make your cloth stay. They spend hundreds and thousands of US dollars to construct out of fibre glass and all sorts of steels materials of this giant cloth pin and people from all over city they comes to see this pin. Nobody things why this cloth pin, someone thinks why cloth pin, we cannot even hang cloth on this pin, you just look at it. It is considered such a wonderful thing, this is considered art, there is no meaning, it is meaningless that it is considered something wonderful. So what is our capacity to create art? But Krishna everything we see including this crazy fellow’s cloth pin, all the sky, all the water, all the species of life, all the flowers, all the plants, all the trees, all the buildings everything has been created by Krishna. All the stars, all the planets and how wonderfully and beautifully he has done. We cannot even imagine the beauty of the spiritual world even this material world is such a work of art that it is inconceivable that anyone could have possibly done it. But Krishna has done it, so Krishna is supreme personality of godhead. When people see the beauty of material existence, if they are intelligent they must except that only an infinite person with infinite artistic abilities could have created this. And only the greatest fool can think that this is all happening by chance or this is all emanating from some impersonalistic energy no. Krishna is the supreme person and that supreme person is the cause of all causes and ultimate goal of life. And he can only be approached through devotion

bhaktyātvananyayāśakya
ahamevaṁ-vidho ’rjuna
jṣātuṁdraṣṭuṁ ca tattvena
praveṣṭuṁ ca paran-tapa

Even in this world we are all Krishna’s parts and parcel, we are all made in image of god and we know that there is nothing more dear to our heart than reciprocation of our love. If someone loves usthat person naturally becomes very dear to our hearts. We cannot avoid reciprocating love with someone who genuinely loves us and thus we are most hard hearted. So Krishna he is the supreme person we are just little persons. And he also finds only pleasure in the reciprocation of love, therefore you can enter into the divine energy of the lord through transcendental knowledge. You can unite with the chit-sakti with the source of all knowledge from within the heart through the process of tapasya into the process of performing many mystical sciences and disciplines but you can only understand the lord and his ultimate supreme features as Krishna the personality of godhead, through bhakti, through devotion, through love.

yeyathāmāṁprapadyante
tāṁstathaivabhajāmyaham
mama vartmānuvartante
manuṣyāḥpārthasarvaśaḥ

As you will approach Krishna he will reciprocate with you accordingly, so therefore devotee is not concerned with anything but the development of love the development of devotion. Often times we tell a story of mystic yogi who came to Prabhupada in Vrindavan and he started showing all these mystical extravagant powers that he had, he started creating things and moving things he was miraculous. Everyone around was thinking oh my god such a wonderful person. I remember one mystic yogi came to new vrindavan and he was talking about different feats of power that he could manifest, exhibit and many of the devotees, they were thinking really you can do like this I don’t believe this, let me see,some of the newer devotees and he started performing his some of the mystic feats, but some of the devotees were very much, the younger devotees were thinking oh my god, it’s wonderful the older devotees were just looking so what? Like they got the doctor of the community and the person was like unh… unh… unh..and he asked how long I should to stop to be said clinically deadand they said twenty minutes. Then he said unh.. unh… unh…and his heart stopsand he was sitting lotus position and there was no pulse no heart beats nothing he was clinically dead, there was not slightest sign of life anywhere in his bodyand there were two doctors who examine him from every point of view of medical technology and there was no sign of life in any part of his body, he was dead. And exactly twenty minutes later he was unh…unh…unh..andhe was just open his eyes and back and people were thinking oh my god and doctors were thinking how was this possible? I have never seen such a thing this beyond our modern science ability to comprehend. And then they had laid him under the car, a truck and ran over the truck and just laid there. None of his limb, nothing were damaged, he was laid there ran over and then they took two cars and put two ropes in both of his hands both cars big American cars they both with full of accelerations were going in both directionhe just held both of them and he was not an strong man, he was just a small man what else he did? All kinds of crazy things. He took a light bulb in his hand and started crushing it up until it was powdered. And there was not any slightest blood, cut or hole in his hand still was like fine salt. He did all kinds things like this. He took a big pole and a big steel beam with pointed edge and he took a huge weight and put it at the end of the beam in the eye and balanced the weight. Totally about a weight of hundred pounds and all the weight was being supported by his eye ball, devotees were thinking my god devotees were thinking he has very strong eye (laughter).

So we were watching, we were thinking Krishna he spent years and years and years of tapasya to do this so what? I mean…so such a yogi who is far greater than this man started exhibiting all his mystic feats to Prabhupada. People were going on to just see how wonderful he was, Prabhupada just looked on that and he said why don’t you give up non-sense of all this and surrender on Krishna. That person realize my god Prabhupada is actually the greatest he knows he knows all the truth that these are so many foolish tricks and he became a life member right then and there, bought whole set of prabhupad books to learn. What it meant to surrender to Krishna. So the mystic power of tiny little jivattama of this world, you may be able to perform few little miracles but Krishna does, no human beings can even attempt to imitate in full. And Krishna he is only attracted by love he is only attracted by devotion.

bhaktyāmāmabhijānāti
yāvānyaścāsmitattvataḥ
tatomāṁtattvatojṣātvā
viśate tad-anantaram

Krishna says it is only by undivided pure devotion that you can understand me as I am. So therefore devotee takes the greatest pleasure not in hearing about himself, not in talking about his own glories, which is so ridiculously insignificant. But he takes the greatest pleasure in hearing the infinite glory of supreme personality of godhead. And then lord appears in such wonderful incarnations as Kuramadev, just to give pleasure to his devotees why else? There are unlimited other ways less extra ordinary, that he could have supported that mountain, as I just said by his will mountain would have supported himself. Why in the world where he appeared as eight hundred thousand miles big tortoise. Just try to… try to fathom the psychology of the god. Why to support the mountain eight hundred thousand miles tortoise. Would you have thought something like that, nobody could think something like that. He exhibits these wonderful pastimes simply to give pleasure to his devotees. It wasn’t to support the mountain, he could have done that in so many ways, but he supported the mountain actually he is the one who made the mountain start sinking. It was not by accident, it was by his will the mountain started sinking. And it was by his will that he exhibit such wonderful form just to give pleasure to thehis devotees, just to have an excuse for all time to come. Everyone will be singing

Kesavadhrtakurmasarirajayajagadisahare !jayajagadisa hare !!jayajagadisa hare!!!

This story took place in satyayuga, took place in millions of millions of years ago. Do you think you can do anything in your life that people will remembering millions of people will be singing your glories everyday millions and millions and millions of years of now. So Krishna appeared in this most wonderful form to give pleasure to us, just to attract our mind not only for the people of that time but people for all times. So we must understand the glory of krishna’s divine incarnations in this light. Krishna says in Gita

paritrāṇāyasādhūnāṁ
vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām
dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya
sambhavāmiyugeyuge

That I appear to annihilate the miscreants to protect the pious and to reestablish the principles of religion. But actually he does not have to do appear personally in these wonderful form to do these things. He can annihilate the miscreants through material nature within a second, he is controller of material nature. He can protect the pious simply by mere his desire, through material nature also he does not have to come personally. He can empower one of his devotees to be guard and protect to reestablish the principle of religion he sends so many of his sons, his prophets. They are also re-establishingthe principles of religions. Why does he come in Kurmarupa? Why does he come in matsyaavtara? Why does he come as varaha dev is a giant bore to hold the earth within his tusks? Why does he come in the form of nrsinghdev ?He could have killed hiranyakashyapujust by just stopping his heart beat,as a parmattama in the heart he just have to do is stop heart beat hiranyakasyapu would have fallen dead. Why did he appear in wonderful magnanimous form as a half man, half lion and he is was just grouling, toring into pieces. He appears for one reason and one reason only in essence. Simply to give pleasure to his devotees, to give his devotees some beautiful, wonderful way of remembering him and attracting their minds. To give all people of all time to come a wonderful… wonderful lila to remember to meditate upon and through this remembrance we develop love for him. Because Krishna is all attractive, more we think of him more we hear his wonderful pastimes, more we understand the transcendental nature. Why he appear the way he appears? The more we naturally become attracted and more our heart opens like a lotus flower blossom with love.

janma karma ca me divyam
evaṁyovettitattvataḥ
tyaktvādehaṁpunarjanma
naitimāmeti so ’rjuna

Krishna says one who understands the transcendental nature of my appearance and activities, he never takes birth in this material world but attains my abode. Because the more we hear the glories of lord from proper sources, if we hear from proper sources and understand the real truth of why and how Krishna appears in these most wonderful forms then simply that sound vibration attracts our consciousness, attracts our heart and we cannot help we cannot resist from loving such wonderful, such a glorious personality Krishna. And when our love for Krishna awakens naturally our desire for sense enjoyment decreases, in fact disappears and in that state we never have to take birth again in this material world, but we return to the eternal abode of the lord. So by it is infinite causeless mercy upon us to attract our mind to attract our hearts and reawaken our love. That Krishna appears in these most …. most wonderful pastimes, most wonderful forms. Just meditate on an eight hundred thousand miles tortoise graciously crawling under this large mountain Mandara and just enjoying the service of the demigods and demons as they are scratching his back. Anjynatsukriti…  Anjynatsukriti…  means you are rendering service without even knowing it, the demigods and demons they are all they were actually churning not as service to lord they were churning out of greed. Especially demons they were churning out of greed, thinking that they would get nectar to enjoy sense enjoyment. But Krishna is so tricky that he arranged in such a way that they were rendering service to him, personal service by massaging his back. And in this way because they were pleasing him by rendering this nice intimate personal service. They were actually making spiritual advancement. So krishna’s only desire for all living beings to become again attracted to him and to engage in his loving service to become purified and to go back to home back to godhead. And that is some and substance, the essence of the reasoning behind the lord’s descend within this material world.

Is there any question?

Yes(……….40.28……)

Answer: Yes…. Through his devotees when you give someone Prasad they are rendering service by simply accepting his mercy in the form of Prasad they are rendering service to Krishna by giving a donation, even if they, even if they are not really exactly understanding what the spiritual significance is? The fact that they are rendering some of their money or some of their time or some of their energy to Krishna, they are rendering service. By hearing the holy name or by speaking the holy name which is emanating from the lips of the great devotees, they are rendering service. There are nine processes of devotional service, one is chanting, one is hearing so in this way. Yes Krishna is incarnated, he incarnates in this world practically speaking through the mercy of his devotees. And their role is very very anxious to engage everyone in the service of the lord knowingly or unknowingly.

Is there any other question?

Thank you very much.
